definition: Entity that creates or trains an AI system, model, or algorithm.
duty_liability_note: Bears design-time obligations: documentation, testing, safety evaluations.
notes: Kept distinct from provider (guide §3, developer/provider flag).

definition: Entity that supplies, distributes, or makes available an AI system or component to others.
duty_liability_note: Supply-chain obligations: documentation pass-through, conformity declarations.
notes: Kept distinct from developer. EU 'provider'≈developer; US frameworks may differ — crosswalk confirms.

definition: Entity that puts an AI system into operation in a specific use context.
duty_liability_note: Deployment-time obligations: impact assessments, user notice, sector compliance.
notes: Sector roles (employer, insurer, hospital, school, platform, publisher, advertiser) map here + separate sector dimension.

definition: Entity that operates or manages a deployed AI system, often on behalf of a deployer.
duty_liability_note: Operational obligations: monitoring, incident response, access controls.
notes: F4 → split from deployer. Kept distinct pending re-harvest data to confirm non-overlap.

definition: Entity in the supply chain that makes an AI product available without substantial modification.
duty_liability_note: Minimal obligations: verify conformity markings, pass-through documentation.
notes: Thin category; kept distinct from provider.

definition: Entity that provides compute infrastructure (cloud, HPC) used to train or run AI systems.
duty_liability_note: Infrastructure-level obligations in some jurisdictions (e.g. reporting large training runs).

definition: Entity that determines the purposes and means of processing personal data.
duty_liability_note: Privacy law primary obligations: lawful basis, data subject rights, DPIAs.

definition: Entity that processes personal data on behalf of and under instruction of a controller.
duty_liability_note: Secondary privacy obligations: DPA requirements, sub-processor chains.

definition: Entity that collects and sells or licenses personal data as a primary business activity.
duty_liability_note: Registration obligations in several states; opt-out and deletion rights.

definition: Government body with enforcement or oversight authority over AI or the regulated domain.
duty_liability_note: Enforcer of obligations, not a duty-bearer; may issue guidance and impose penalties.
orrick_terms: agency, regulator, commission, attorney general, enforcement authority
notes: F2 → split from government umbrella. Enforcer/oversight bodies only.

definition: Government body that procures, uses, or deploys AI (not primarily an enforcement body).
duty_liability_note: Government-as-regulated-actor obligations: procurement rules, algorithmic audits.
notes: F2 → split from government umbrella. Government-as-deployer/user, not enforcer.

definition: Natural person — consumer, applicant, user, minor — who is a subject or rights-holder under the law.
duty_liability_note: Protected party, not a duty-bearer. actor_scope=protected applies.
notes: F3 → kept as own code AND retains actor_scope=protected flag (both).

definition: Generic catch-all for covered or regulated entities that do not fit a more specific code.
duty_liability_note: Catch-all only — obligations depend on the law's specific scope language.
orrick_terms: covered entity, regulated entity, third party
notes: Routing target for unresolved terms until LKA ratifies more specific mapping.
